calculateHammingDistance.name: Calculate the Hamming Distance
calculateHammingDistance.description: Write a function that calculates the Hamming distance between two DNA strands of equal length. The Hamming distance is the number of positions where the corresponding characters differ.

hammingEmptyStrands.name: Empty strands
hammingEmptyStrands.description: Empty strands have a Hamming distance of 0.

hammingSingleLetterIdentical.name: Single letter identical strands
hammingSingleLetterIdentical.description: Single-letter identical strands have a Hamming distance of 0.

hammingLongIdenticalStrands.name: Long identical strands
hammingLongIdenticalStrands.description: Long identical strands have a Hamming distance of 0.

hammingSingleLetterDifferent.name: Single letter different strands
hammingSingleLetterDifferent.description: Single-letter different strands have a Hamming distance of 1.

hammingLongDifferentStrands.name: Long different strands
hammingLongDifferentStrands.description: Long strands with differences have a calculated Hamming distance.

hammingDistanceMeaning.question: What does 'Hamming distance' mean in plain terms?
hammingDistanceMeaning.answer: It's the number of positions where the two strings differ. So 'GAGA' vs 'GATA' differ at position 3 only, giving a distance of 1.

compareCharactersAtPosition.question: How do I compare characters at the same position in both strings?
compareCharactersAtPosition.answer: Loop through one string with a position counter. At each step, look at the character at that position in the first string and the character at the same position in the second.

trackDifferenceCount.question: How do I track 'how many differences'?
trackDifferenceCount.answer: Keep a counter starting at 0. Each time you find a position where the characters don't match, add 1. Return the counter at the end.
